titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/29

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=284 status=live modified=2021-04-06 09:53:25.590212+00 | translation moa=285 status=live modified=2021-04-06 09:53:21.896797+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Dunecanus filius Gilleberti filii Fergus Salutem . Sciatis me concessisse et dedisse
et hac mea Carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie sancte Marie de Melr’ et monachis ibidem deo seuientibus pro animabus dauid et Melcolmi
Regum dominorum meorum . et Comitis . Henrici . et pro salute domini mei Willelmi Regis . et Comitis . dauid . fratris eius . et Comitis . Dun’ec’ . et pro salute mea et
animabus patris mei et matris mee . et omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum in liberam et puram et perpetuam Elemosinam totam terram
de Moybothelbeg per has diuisas . Scilicet a capite burne que descendit de Crumder deorsum usque in Culelungford et sicut
eadem burna descendit de Culelungford in polnetib’ . et sicut polnetib’ cadit in Don . et per Don sursum usque ubi polgarroh
cadit in Don . et sic sursum per polnegarrah usque ad paruuam mussam . et inde usque sicum quendam qui descendit ex australi par
te eiusdem musse in polnegarrah . et per eundem sicum sursum usque ubi idem sicus ascendit in polnecogi . et polnecogi ascendit in
Duuah . et inde per sicum sursum usque ad uiam que uocatur Enahconecal . et per eandem uiam deorsum usque ad burnam que descendit
de predicto Crumder . Preterea concessi et dedi et hac mea Carta confirmaui predictis monachis et Ecclesie de Melr’ totam terram de Be
thoc in liberam et puram et perpetuam Elemosinam per has diuisas . Scilicet a Lemenelung uersus orientem per uiam usque ad Neskecoke
ri . et inde uersus orientem usque ad magnam massam de salicibus . et inde deorsum ex aquilonali parte Croah parui usque ad Gallan .
et inde uersus orientem usque ad sicum qui uadit ex aquilonali parte nigre rupis . et per eundem sicum usque ad locum qui uocatur Tunregaith .
ubi de ipso sico exit Riuulus quidam qui dicitur Polnesalahari . et inde usque ubi idem Riuulus cadit in polnetyberes . Totam
hanc terram tam illam de Moybothelbeg quam istam de Bethoc cum omnibus pertinentiis suis . et quicquid continetur infra prenomina
tas diuisas in bosco et plano . in pratis et pasturis . in uiis et semitis . in moris et mariscis . in terris et aquis et piscariis
et in Omnibus aliis rebus et aisiamentis dedi predictis monachis et Ecclesie de Melr’ tenenda et possidenda in perpetuum ita libere
et quiete plenarie et honorifice et absolute ab omni terreno seruitio et consuetudine et exactione seculari sicut aliquam Elemo
sinam liberius et quietius plenius et honorificentius tenent uel possident . ita tamen quod annis singulis dabunt mihi et heredibus meis tantum
duas Marcas argenti ad recognitionem pro terra de Bethoc . unam scilicet Marcam . ad pentecosten . et unam ad festum sancti martini .
Et ipsi monachi utentur omnibus prenominatis ad libitum suum modis omnibus sicut melius et utilius sibi et suis uisum fuerit . et ego
et heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabimus eis hec omnia contra omnes homines inperpetuum et adquietabimus de omni seruitio .
Et quia uolui ut hec mea Elemosina firma esset et stabilis et nullo modo posset in irritum duci horum omnium testes et fideiussores posu
i Episcopum et Ecclesiam Glasguensem . ut si aliquando ego uel heredes mei a tenore huius Carte deuiauerimus . ipsa Ecclesia et eius pontifi
ces per ecclesiasticam censuram ad correctionem nos reuocent . Hiis testibus . Domino Iocelino . Glasguensi . Episcopo . Willelmo Abbate de
sancta cruce . Simone archidiacono . Comite . Dunecano . Malcolmo filio eius . Comite . Gilleb’ . Eth filio Comite . de Leuen’ . Rog’ . de
Scalebroc . Gilleasald mac Gilleandres . Gillenem mac Coleman . Edgaro Camerario meo . Gillecrist Bretnach .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Duncan son of Gilbert son of Fergus, Greeting: Know that I have granted and given and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God there, for the souls of my lords, the kings David and Malcolm, and of Earl Henry, and for the salvation of my lord, King William, and of Earl David his brother, and of Earl Duncan, and for my own salvation and the souls of my father and my mother, and of all my ancestors and successors, in free and pure and perpetual alms, the whole land of Maybole-beg by these boundaries: That is, from the head of the burn that descends from Crumder downwards as far as Culelungford and as the same burn descends from Culelungford to Polnetib’, and as Polnetib’ falls into the Doon, and by the Doon upwards as far as where Polgarroh falls into the Doon, and so upwards by the Polnegarrah as far as the small peat bog, and thence as far as a syke which goes down from the south side of the same peat bog to Polnegarrah, and by the same syke upwards as far as where the same syke goes up to Polnecogi, and Polnecogi ascends to Duuah, and thence by the syke upwards as far as the road which is called Enahconecal, and by the same road downwards as far as the burn that descends from the said Crumder. In addition, I have granted and given and by this my charter made firm to the said monks and church of Melrose the whole land of Beoch in free and pure and perpetual alms by these boundaries, that is, from Lemenelung eastwards by the road as far as Neskecokeri and from there eastwards as far as a great spread of willows, and thence downwards from the northern side of Croah paruus to Gallan, and from there eastwards as far as a syke which goes from the northern side of the black rock, and from the same syke as far the place which is called Tundregaith, where from this syke a certain stream, which is called Polnesalahart, comes out, and from there as far as where the same stream falls into the Polnetyberes. This whole land as well that of Maybole-beg as that of Beoch, with all their pertinents, and whatever is contained within the said boundaries in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in roads and paths, in moors and marshes, in lands and waters and fisheries, and in all other things and easements, I have given to the said monks and the church of Melrose to be held and possessed in perpetuity as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, and loosed from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction as they hold or possess any alms freely and quietly, fully and honourably, provided that they shall give to me and my heirs each year no more than two merks of silver as a recognition in return for the land of Beoch, that is, one merk at Pentecost, and one at the feast of St Martin. And the monks shall make use of all the aforementioned at their pleasure, by all means, just as it seems good and useful to them and their men. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant these things to them against all men for ever and we shall discharge them from all service. And because I have willed that this my alms be firm and secure and that it could by no means be brought to nought, I have placed the bishop and church of Glasgow as witnesses and sureties of these things, so that if at any time I or my heirs stray from the purport of this charter, that church and its bishop may call us back to correction by ecclesiastical censure. With these witnesses, the lord Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow, William abbot of Holyrood, Simon the archdeacon, Earl Duncan, Malcolm his son, Earl Gilbert, Aed the son of the earl of Lennox, Roger of Skelbrooke, Gilla Asaild mac Gillanders, Gilla na Naem mac Colman, Edgar my chamberlain, Gilchrist Bretnach.
